rules.summary: A copied structure may be referenced.
A reference may be copied as a reference, but this does not copy the referenced structure.
A projection may be collapsed into a reduced representation.
Collapsed structure may be expanded only if expansion rules preserve or reconstruct enough structure.
A split followed by merge may resemble reversal, but only if distinctions are recoverable.
A merge followed by split may recover components only when provenance or partition criteria are retained.
Binding followed by unbinding may restore prior dependency state if no other transformations depend on the binding.
Unbinding followed by rebinding may restore dependency only if the same constraint is reapplied.
A versioned successor may branch into multiple continuities.
Each branch may later receive versioned successor states.
An authorized transformation pathway may be attested.
An attestation may support authorization, but does not itself authorize.
